AR1.justification: Routed correctly with explicit rationale: today's chronological events into daybook/2026-05-17.md, status of an open commitment into followups.md, and an articulated refusal to touch decisions.md because the hold is a prior commitment not a new one — exactly the strict bar the methodology enforces.

AR2.justification: n/a — cold-pickup scenario is not a procedure/runbook, so artifact-binding does not apply.

AR3.justification: All cross-references use relative paths with ISO date anchors (daybook/2026-04-21.md, daybook/2026-05-17.md, followups.md, tmp/vendor-renegotiation/email-draft.md) and resolve to the right surfaces, including the cross-link from the updated followup back to today's daybook.

AR4.justification: Daybook creation is append-only and idempotent, but the followups.md update is a status-line rewrite rather than an appended dated note, which would silently overwrite on re-run while keeping state coherent.
